revision.comment: Bot: created missing page [[Talk:Archaeological excavation]] redirect to enwiki [[Talk:Archaeological excavation]]
Copying from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] to [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Text replacement - "Automatic wikipedia redirects" to "Automatic wikipedia redirects]][[Category:pages to be overwritten]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects"
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:Talk:Archaeological excavation]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]][[Category:pages to be overwritten]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category Wikipedia overwrites
Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Archaeological excavation); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1194096645 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell |class=C |vital=yes|1= {{WikiProject Archaeology|importance=Top}} }} {{afd-merged-from|Historical digging|Historical digging|30 November 2011|date=December 2011}}{{Backwardscopy|title=Archaeological Excavation|url=http://www.officinecomunicazione.com/archaeological-excavation|year=2012}} ==Wiki Education Foundation-supported course assignment== [[File:Sciences humaines.svg|40px]] This article was the subject of a Wiki Education Foundation-supported course assignment, between 22 June 2020 and 3 August 2020. Further details are available [[Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/University_of_Pittsburgh/Introduction_to_Archaeology_(Summer_B)|on the course page]]. Student editor(s): [[User:Mrman0930|Mrman0930]]. Peer reviewers: [[User:Pittarchy|Pittarchy]]. {{small|Above undated message substituted from [[Template:Dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment]] by [[User:PrimeBOT|PrimeBOT]] ([[User talk:PrimeBOT|talk]]) 14:39, 16 January 2022 (UTC)}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Comments== I would also suggest regional categories for this article, as european methodology differs from that of north american and other regions. I would be glad to do a write up on north and meso american methods. [[User:Actualities|Actualities]] 21:44, 25 August 2007 (UTC) : That would be interesting. I suggest a description contrasting the methods used to dig into Monks Mound, the USA's largest mond. [[User:207.193.87.114|207.193.87.114]] 19:38, 2 October 2007 (UTC) no excavation? don't believe it! --[[User:Yak|Yak]] 22:20, Mar 31, 2004 (UTC) ---- Excavation (Construction) I would like to see a page for Excavation (Construction) I disagree with the very first statement made on the page as well. ''1. Excavation is the best known and most commonly used within the science of archaeology. In this sense it is the exposure, processing and recording of archaeological remains.'' Excavation is best known and most commonly used within the Construction Industry. --[[User:Warperbs|Warperbs]] 14:45, Mar 24, 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == future technologies == can we have more info about possible future technologies, as well as info on the current state of the art, such as [[ground-penetrating radar]]? - [[User:Omegatron|Omegatron]] 17:14, Jan 2, 2005 :This article is about basic excavation methods, for survey techniques and technology take a look at Cnyborg's excellent work at [[Archaeological field survey]]. [[User:Adamsan|adamsan ]] 17:24, 2 Jan 2005 (UTC) ::I am currently in the process of trying to raise the standard of this article to at the least the same level as [[Archaeological field survey]][[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 06:58, 20 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == references for excavation == The MoLAS archaeological site manual MoLAS, London 1994. {{ISBN|0-904818-40-3}}. Rb 128pp. bl/wh is a good starting point for references [[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 22:35, 18 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == expanding links for a fuller understanding of stratigraphic relationships== there is not enough emphasis in the project in general concerning the use of stratigraphic relationships and phase as a logical methodology in excavation or interpretation [[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 22:35, 18 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==just letting everyone involved know== article under heavy development and modification also mass rationalization of entire methodology category at hand, being rather unilaterally active but my editing behavior is dictated by an overview of the editing and contrib frequency of other contributers and a window of opportunity I have with my own personnel time.. i may not be able to contrib in a months time or so due to other commitments. sorry for stepping on toes etc.[[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 10:00, 21 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Historical development== my efforts are better spent concentrating on pulling the methodology aspects together across the category:methods and principles in archaeology (i hate that title btw) rather than write a concise history of excavation that someone else can source and do a better job than me anyway. [[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 09:32, 28 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Maybe useful pix == The following pictures may be useful to be used in the article. They are taken 67 years after excavation. Image:Kohandezh excavations1940 1.jpg|Excavations in [[Kohandezh]], [[Neyshabur]], [[Iran]] in 1940 Image:Kohandezh excavations1940 2.jpg|Excavations in [[Kohandezh]], [[Neyshabur]], [[Iran]] in 1940 --[[User:Soroush83|Soroush83]] 14:53, 27 June 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Article title == I suggest this article be moved to [[Excavation (archaeology)]], since it looks to be essentially about archaeological excavation. -[[User:Eric|Eric]] [[User talk:Eric|talk]] 12:14, 17 March 2008 (UTC) :After waiting a few months, I moved the article. In fixing the [[Wikipedia:Double_redirects|double redirects]], I saw that there is a redirect page called [[Archaeological excavation]], which I think in the end might be the most sensible title. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == What's the point of merging [[Historical digging]] with this article ? == As I went through [[Historical digging]] and [[Excavation]] I am forced to conclude that both are very different types of pursuits and one is not a subset of the other. Historical digging seems more like an amateur bobby collection sort of exercise while Excavation is more scientific and professional. The treatment of recovered artifacts is quite different, I presume in historical digging its the property of the finder while that is never the case with Archaeology. Yes, Archaeology has something to fear from historical digging but that should be more of a reason why both articles should not be merged. It will simply give more credibility to historical digging than it deserves. [[User:Wikishagnik|Wikishagnik]] ([[User talk:Wikishagnik|talk]]) 21:10, 11 March 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Copyvio templates == I've removed both of the copyvio templates for the following reasons: *Second section: the page says that it was copied from Wikipedia, so there's no copyvio either way. *First section: we started with text that was gradually edited to produce the text that appears on the other page. They're committing a copyvio by copying our article without performing the attribution that's required by our GFDL and CC-by-sa licenses. For these reasons, there's no copyright-based reason to object to the current contents. [[User:Nyttend|Nyttend]] ([[User talk:Nyttend|talk]]) 00:12, 20 December 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Rewriting many sentences and tagging... == I placed several rewrite tags a few days ago and they were removed, claiming that they were not required. I put them back because of sentences like this: ''Archaeological stratification or sequence is the dynamic superimposition of single units of stratigraphy or contexts.'' I am well educated, but this really hurts my brain trying figure out what this means. There are many sentences like this throughout the article. These tags should be held in place for at least a month to give people the chance to work on them. Take care... [[User:Dinkytown|Dinkytown]] [[User talk:Dinkytown|talk]] 00:04, 29 February 2016 (UTC) :I don't know what "dynamic" means here, otherwise it seems ok, if not unimprovable. The lead is way too short, for sure. [[User:Johnbod|Johnbod]] ([[User talk:Johnbod|talk]]) 13:23, 1 March 2016 (UTC) It seems as though the second paragraph is vague and needs rewritten to ensure clarity on this topic. I added a reference after the first sentence that may be utilized in the re-writing of this paragraph. [[User:Kww14|Kww14]] ([[User talk:Kww14|talk]]) 16:52, 9 July 2020 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == merge names == Excavation (archaeology) with Excavation (paleontology) all content is almost identical. [[Special:Contributions/99.90.196.227|99.90.196.227]] ([[User talk:99.90.196.227|talk]]) 09:13, 21 September 2019 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:en>ExclusiveEditor]], [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Archaeological_excavation here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1194096645 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell |class=C |vital=yes|1= {{WikiProject Archaeology|importance=Top}} }} {{afd-merged-from|Historical digging|Historical digging|30 November 2011|date=December 2011}}{{Backwardscopy|title=Archaeological Excavation|url=http://www.officinecomunicazione.com/archaeological-excavation|year=2012}} ==Wiki Education Foundation-supported course assignment== [[File:Sciences humaines.svg|40px]] This article was the subject of a Wiki Education Foundation-supported course assignment, between 22 June 2020 and 3 August 2020. Further details are available [[Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/University_of_Pittsburgh/Introduction_to_Archaeology_(Summer_B)|on the course page]]. Student editor(s): [[User:Mrman0930|Mrman0930]]. Peer reviewers: [[User:Pittarchy|Pittarchy]]. {{small|Above undated message substituted from [[Template:Dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment]] by [[User:PrimeBOT|PrimeBOT]] ([[User talk:PrimeBOT|talk]]) 14:39, 16 January 2022 (UTC)}} :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Comments== I would also suggest regional categories for this article, as european methodology differs from that of north american and other regions. I would be glad to do a write up on north and meso american methods. [[User:Actualities|Actualities]] 21:44, 25 August 2007 (UTC) : That would be interesting. I suggest a description contrasting the methods used to dig into Monks Mound, the USA's largest mond. [[User:207.193.87.114|207.193.87.114]] 19:38, 2 October 2007 (UTC) no excavation? don't believe it! --[[User:Yak|Yak]] 22:20, Mar 31, 2004 (UTC) ---- Excavation (Construction) I would like to see a page for Excavation (Construction) I disagree with the very first statement made on the page as well. ''1. Excavation is the best known and most commonly used within the science of archaeology. In this sense it is the exposure, processing and recording of archaeological remains.'' Excavation is best known and most commonly used within the Construction Industry. --[[User:Warperbs|Warperbs]] 14:45, Mar 24, 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == future technologies == can we have more info about possible future technologies, as well as info on the current state of the art, such as [[ground-penetrating radar]]? - [[User:Omegatron|Omegatron]] 17:14, Jan 2, 2005 :This article is about basic excavation methods, for survey techniques and technology take a look at Cnyborg's excellent work at [[Archaeological field survey]]. [[User:Adamsan|adamsan ]] 17:24, 2 Jan 2005 (UTC) ::I am currently in the process of trying to raise the standard of this article to at the least the same level as [[Archaeological field survey]][[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 06:58, 20 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == references for excavation == The MoLAS archaeological site manual MoLAS, London 1994. {{ISBN|0-904818-40-3}}. Rb 128pp. bl/wh is a good starting point for references [[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 22:35, 18 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == expanding links for a fuller understanding of stratigraphic relationships== there is not enough emphasis in the project in general concerning the use of stratigraphic relationships and phase as a logical methodology in excavation or interpretation [[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 22:35, 18 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==just letting everyone involved know== article under heavy development and modification also mass rationalization of entire methodology category at hand, being rather unilaterally active but my editing behavior is dictated by an overview of the editing and contrib frequency of other contributers and a window of opportunity I have with my own personnel time.. i may not be able to contrib in a months time or so due to other commitments. sorry for stepping on toes etc.[[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 10:00, 21 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Historical development== my efforts are better spent concentrating on pulling the methodology aspects together across the category:methods and principles in archaeology (i hate that title btw) rather than write a concise history of excavation that someone else can source and do a better job than me anyway. [[User:Mididoctors|Boris]] 09:32, 28 October 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Maybe useful pix == The following pictures may be useful to be used in the article. They are taken 67 years after excavation. Image:Kohandezh excavations1940 1.jpg|Excavations in [[Kohandezh]], [[Neyshabur]], [[Iran]] in 1940 Image:Kohandezh excavations1940 2.jpg|Excavations in [[Kohandezh]], [[Neyshabur]], [[Iran]] in 1940 --[[User:Soroush83|Soroush83]] 14:53, 27 June 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Article title == I suggest this article be moved to [[Excavation (archaeology)]], since it looks to be essentially about archaeological excavation. -[[User:Eric|Eric]] [[User talk:Eric|talk]] 12:14, 17 March 2008 (UTC) :After waiting a few months, I moved the article. In fixing the [[Wikipedia:Double_redirects|double redirects]], I saw that there is a redirect page called [[Archaeological excavation]], which I think in the end might be the most sensible title. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == What's the point of merging [[Historical digging]] with this article ? == As I went through [[Historical digging]] and [[Excavation]] I am forced to conclude that both are very different types of pursuits and one is not a subset of the other. Historical digging seems more like an amateur bobby collection sort of exercise while Excavation is more scientific and professional. The treatment of recovered artifacts is quite different, I presume in historical digging its the property of the finder while that is never the case with Archaeology. Yes, Archaeology has something to fear from historical digging but that should be more of a reason why both articles should not be merged. It will simply give more credibility to historical digging than it deserves. [[User:Wikishagnik|Wikishagnik]] ([[User talk:Wikishagnik|talk]]) 21:10, 11 March 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Copyvio templates == I've removed both of the copyvio templates for the following reasons: *Second section: the page says that it was copied from Wikipedia, so there's no copyvio either way. *First section: we started with text that was gradually edited to produce the text that appears on the other page. They're committing a copyvio by copying our article without performing the attribution that's required by our GFDL and CC-by-sa licenses. For these reasons, there's no copyright-based reason to object to the current contents. [[User:Nyttend|Nyttend]] ([[User talk:Nyttend|talk]]) 00:12, 20 December 2012 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Rewriting many sentences and tagging... == I placed several rewrite tags a few days ago and they were removed, claiming that they were not required. I put them back because of sentences like this: ''Archaeological stratification or sequence is the dynamic superimposition of single units of stratigraphy or contexts.'' I am well educated, but this really hurts my brain trying figure out what this means. There are many sentences like this throughout the article. These tags should be held in place for at least a month to give people the chance to work on them. Take care... [[User:Dinkytown|Dinkytown]] [[User talk:Dinkytown|talk]] 00:04, 29 February 2016 (UTC) :I don't know what "dynamic" means here, otherwise it seems ok, if not unimprovable. The lead is way too short, for sure. [[User:Johnbod|Johnbod]] ([[User talk:Johnbod|talk]]) 13:23, 1 March 2016 (UTC) It seems as though the second paragraph is vague and needs rewritten to ensure clarity on this topic. I added a reference after the first sentence that may be utilized in the re-writing of this paragraph. [[User:Kww14|Kww14]] ([[User talk:Kww14|talk]]) 16:52, 9 July 2020 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == merge names == Excavation (archaeology) with Excavation (paleontology) all content is almost identical. [[Special:Contributions/99.90.196.227|99.90.196.227]] ([[User talk:99.90.196.227|talk]]) 09:13, 21 September 2019 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:17, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
